pi_items.source: Verified from Wazuh standard ruleset documentation which defines rule 5402 as 'Successful sudo to ROOT executed', explicitly matching the task instructions.
Task description strictly mandates creating a new custom child rule with ID 100100.
Task description strictly mandates suppressing alerts for the 'backup_svc' user.
Verified from Wazuh standard ruleset documentation which defines rule 5501 as 'PAM: Login session opened', matching the parent rule requirement in the task instructions.
Standard Wazuh local rules file path, explicitly enforced by the task description and verifier.py evaluation logic.

privileged_info_summary: I have audited the task and confirmed it requires modifying the standard Wazuh local rules configuration. Verified via Wazuh documentation that rule 5402 corresponds to 'Successful sudo to ROOT executed' and rule 5501 corresponds to 'PAM: Login session opened'. The target custom child rule ID (100100), target user ('backup_svc'), and target file path ('/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml') exactly match the explicit instructions provided in the task description and are dynamically evaluated via verifier.py logic.
